title: Present perfect or past simple?
explanation:
Present perfect or past simple?

Past simple — finished time (yesterday, last year, in 2010, ago).

Present perfect — unfinished time (today, this week), no specific time, or connection with now (since, for, yet, already, ever).

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option to complete the sentences.

questions.text: I [*saw|have seen|see] her at the party last night.
We [*visited|have visited|visit] Italy three years ago.
They [*have already done|already did|already do] their homework.
He [*didn't go|hasn't gone|doesn't go] to work yesterday.
[*Have you ever eaten|Did you ever eat|Do you ever eat] sushi?

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: I ____ him this morning. | *have seen | saw | see
She ____ to Paris in 2018. | *went | has gone | goes
We ____ each other for ten years. | *have known | knew | know
They ____ the film yet. | *haven't watched | didn't watch | don't watch

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option to complete the sentences.

questions.text: I [*have lost|lost|lose] my passport. I can't find it anywhere.
She [*graduated|has graduated|graduates] from university last June.
We [*have lived|lived|live] in this flat for two months.
[*Did|Have|Do] they finish the project on Friday?
He [*has never tried|never tried|never tries] skiing.
